title: Ep 149: TSMC Q4 25 Earnings, OpenAI Needs more Compute and Monetization
description: In this episode of The Circuit, Ben Bajarin and Jay Goldberg discuss the launch of Ben's new publication, "The Diligent Stack." The duo then performs a deep dive into TSMC’s recent earnings, analyzing the risks of semiconductor cyclicality, the massive CapEx requirements for the future, and the specific bottlenecks in advanced packaging (CoWoS). Later, they shift focus to OpenAI’s partnership with Cerebras and the introduction of ads to fund massive compute needs. Finally, they break down the la

title: US-Taiwan Trade Deal, TSMC's Wendell Huang on Q4 Earnings
description:
The US and Taiwan agreed to a trade pact that would lower tariffs on goods from Taiwan to 15% and see Taiwanese semiconductor companies increase financing for American operations by $500 billion.
 Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. shares climb as much as 2.7% in Taipei after the chipmaker’s strong results and outlook underscored the strength of demand trends related to AI. The shares ended 4.4% higher overnight in the US. Rob Haworth, Senior Investment Strategy Director at U.S. Bank Asset M

title: TSMC Earnings Q4 2025 | „KI ist real“ Die 56-Milliarden-Dollar-Offensive
description:
TSMC bestätigt eindrucksvoll: Der KI-Boom ist kein Hype, sondern ein langlebiger Megatrend. In dieser Folge analysieren wir die Rekordzahlen des vierten Quartals mit einem Umsatzsprung von 20,5 % auf 33,7 Milliarden Dollar.
Wir diskutieren, warum CEO C.C. Wei trotz Sorgen um eine Blase zuversichtlich bleibt und die Kapitalausgaben (CapEx) für 2026 drastisch auf 52 bis 56 Milliarden Dollar anhebt,. Erfahren Sie, warum die 2-Nanometer-Technologie schneller hochfährt als erwartet und wie TSMC plant

title: Acid Breath. Inside the Macro Storm: Fed Policy, TSMC Earnings, and the Next Credit Crunch
description:
Send us Fan Mail
There’s a smell in the air, that faint electric tang before a storm. The markets are choppy, the screens green and red in alternating flickers, and behind it all you can almost hear the static of something old returning.
In this episode of Acid Breath, I drive straight through the fog. The Philadelphia Fed survey just recorded its steepest drop since 2020; the factory heartbeat of America skipping hard. Across the Pacific, Taiwan Semiconductor printed perfection: record profits,

title: TSMC Earnings: AI Supply Chain Winners and Losers
description:
TSMC Q3 revenue jumps to $32.47B, beating forecasts on surging AI demand. Customers like $NVDA and $AAPL helped drive the beat; full Q3 earnings arrive Oct 16. 

Winners - AI Chip Designers
Reason: Stronger TSMC output at leading nodes (N5/N3) supports higher shipments of AI accelerators and CPUs, easing supply friction and sustaining pricing power. 
Names: $NVDA, $AMD

Winners - Memory and AI Server Suppliers
Reason: AI demand needs High-Bandwidth Memory and accelerated server builds; a robust

title: TSMC Earnings Preview, Stocks Set Fresh Record on AI
description:
TSMC is poised to release its Q3 earnings report next week. The company is likely to excel, with both revenue and gross margins expected to defeat consensus supported by strong July-August sales data. TSMC is due to release its September sales data and the figures could offer some insight into wider semiconductor demand. We turn to Debby Wu, Team Leader for North Asia Technology at Bloomberg News.
 Plus, US stocks closed at a record on Wednesday, thanks to shares benefiting from the artificial-i

title: EP: 126 NVIDIA/AMD Back in China! TSMC Earnings, and Agentic AI Causes Total Infra Upgrade
description: In this episode, Ben Bajarin and Jay Goldberg discuss the recent developments in the AI and semiconductor industries, focusing on Nvidia and AMD's approval to sell modified chipsets to China, the political dynamics influencing these decisions, and the competitive landscape with Huawei. They also analyze TSMC's earnings, the need for infrastructure upgrades to support AI agents, and the implications of Synopsys' acquisition of Ansys. The conversation concludes with a look at Marvell's leadership

title: Morning Brief: President Tump Announces 25% Tariffs on Imported Chips, Rare Earth Stocks Surge, TSMC to Report Earnings Today (1/15/26)
description:
President Trump announced 25% tariffs on imported chips not used for U.S. AI, targeting re-exports and aiming for billions in revenue.
The Supreme Court deferred rulings on Trump-era tariffs. An executive order aims to secure the national rare earth supply.
Reports suggest de-escalation in Iran, causing oil price drops. The Fed's Beige Book indicated moderate economic expansion with persistent inflation.

title: TSM Stock Analysis: Navigating Semiconductor Trends and Forecasts
show: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Industry News
description:
